mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production signal expanding economic output, which raises corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 prices higher over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward return Rank IC with INDPRO level changes is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.5%, labor market tightness boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6.5%, the proportion of positive 60-day S&P 500 returns is less than 50% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low inflation and low policy rates reduces discount rates and supports equity valuations; the joint effect of stable prices and accommodative monetary policy is positive for S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: A composite score (e.g., low FEDFUNDS and low CPIAUCSL) fails to rank-correlate positively with 20-day forward returns (Spearman > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: During the recovery phase of the business cycle, rising retail and food services sales signal strengthening consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue and S&P 500 gains over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods classified as recovery (e.g., GDP growth > 0% and UNRATE above 6%), positive RSAFS changes do not lead to above-median 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 1,000 thousand units indicates a robust housing market, which stimulates construction activity, employment, and consumer wealth, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1,000, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) in a sample of at least 20 observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales signals broad economic strength; the joint expansion of labor income and consumer spending propels corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: In months when both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase sequentially, the median 20-day S&P 500 return does not exceed the all-period median return by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A declining consumer price index signals disinflation, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and raising present values, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly negative changes in CPIAUCSL do not precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with an information coefficient above 0.05 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In an early recovery regime with unemployment above 6%, a declining unemployment rate accelerates labor market healing, boosts disposable income, and raises equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: During early recovery periods (GDP growth > 2% and UNRATE > 6%), decreasing UNRATE is associated with negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or a negative rank correlation.
